description: 'Update' is declared in allowed-tools but is not a recognized Claude Code tool; likely a leftover from an early template draft
suggested_fix: Remove 'Update' from the allowed-tools list; use 'Write' or 'Edit' for update operations

description: 'Update' is declared in allowed-tools but is not a recognized Claude Code tool
suggested_fix: Remove 'Update' from the allowed-tools list

description: 'Update' is declared in allowed-tools but is not a recognized Claude Code tool
suggested_fix: Remove 'Update' from the allowed-tools list

description: Command takes required argument $1 (feature-name) but provides no guard or error message when $1 is empty or missing
suggested_fix: Add a check at the top: 'If $1 is empty, stop with: Run /kiro:spec-init <feature-name> first'

description: Vague quantifiers throughout 460-line body: 'comprehensive', 'thorough', 'appropriate', 'relevant', 'key' appear 10+ times; penalty capped at -20
suggested_fix: Replace vague terms with measurable criteria (e.g., 'comprehensive' → 'covering all EARS requirements from requirements.md')

description: Command takes required argument $1 (feature-name) but provides no guard when $1 is empty
suggested_fix: Add a guard at the top: if spec files for $1 do not exist, stop with a clear error message

description: Command uses $ARGUMENTS (project description) but does not guard against an empty argument string
suggested_fix: Add: 'If $ARGUMENTS is empty, stop with: Provide a project description, e.g. /kiro:spec-init My feature description'

description: Mild vague language: 'clear next step' (line 80), 'brief description' (line 82) (~2 instances)
suggested_fix: Use concrete phrasing e.g. 'next command to run' instead of 'clear next step'

description: Command takes required $1 but no guard for empty or missing argument
suggested_fix: Add a prerequisites check that verifies $1 is non-empty before running bash inline commands

description: 'comprehensive' in description, 'appropriate subject' (line 35), 'concise' (line 36) — ~3 vague instances
suggested_fix: Replace 'appropriate subject' with a specific decision tree (e.g. 'for software projects use the service name; for processes use the team name')

description: Command takes required $1 but no guard for empty argument; bash inline commands on lines 13-22 would silently fail or expose unexpected directory listings
suggested_fix: Validate $1 is non-empty and matches an existing spec directory before executing bash inline commands

description: 'comprehensive status report' (line 24), 'appropriate' (line 73), 'clear' (line 79) — ~4 vague instances
suggested_fix: Replace 'comprehensive' with explicit list of sections; replace 'appropriate' granularity with specific size thresholds

description: Command takes required $1 but no guard for empty argument
suggested_fix: Add prerequisites block: verify $1 is set and .kiro/specs/$1/design.md exists before proceeding

description: 'comprehensive context' (line 30), 'appropriate' (line 72), 'relevant' (lines 76, 108) — ~4 vague instances
suggested_fix: Replace 'comprehensive context' with an explicit list of files to load; remove or quantify 'appropriate'

description: 'comprehensive' x4, 'significant' x4, 'relevant' x2, 'major' x3 — 13+ vague instances, penalty capped at -20
suggested_fix: Audit each vague quantifier and replace with a concrete criterion or measurable threshold

description: Command takes required $1 but no guard for empty argument
suggested_fix: Check .kiro/specs/$1/design.md exists at the top; if not, emit the already-documented error message

description: 'acceptable' (line 116), 'manageable' (line 116), 'significant' (line 115), 'thorough' (line 88), 'appropriate' (line 164) — ~5 vague instances
suggested_fix: Define concrete thresholds: replace 'acceptable risk' with named risk levels; replace 'manageable' with a specific criterion

description: Command takes required $1 but no guard for empty argument; bash inline on line 21 would expose directory listing errors
suggested_fix: Validate $1 before executing inline bash commands; add error message directing user to run spec-init first

description: 'comprehensive' (line 7), 'relevant' (lines 35, 40), 'appropriate' (line 95) — ~5 vague instances
suggested_fix: Replace vague terms with measurable criteria aligned to the EARS requirement format the command is analyzing

description: 'appropriate' (line 82), 'clear' (line 98), 'unique value' (line 150), 'relevant' (line 102) — ~4 vague instances
suggested_fix: Replace 'appropriate' with concrete decision criteria for each inclusion mode

description: 'focused updates' (line 5), 'rapid feedback' (line 10), 'signal-rich' (line 30) — ~3 mild vague instances
suggested_fix: Minimal impact; 'rapid feedback' could specify 'sub-second recompile' if a concrete latency target is desired

description: !bash -c 'ls -la .kiro/specs/$1/' embeds the user-supplied slash-command argument directly into a bash -c string without sanitization; a crafted feature name containing shell metacharacters executes arbitrary commands
suggested_fix: Validate $1 matches ^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+$ before any bash inline; use 'ls -la -- .kiro/specs/$1/' with the -- separator as a secondary defense

description: !bash -c 'ls -la .kiro/specs/$1/ 2>/dev/null || echo ...' embeds user argument in bash -c without sanitization
suggested_fix: Sanitize $1 against ^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+$ before inline shell commands; add -- to ls invocation

description: !bash -c 'ls -la .kiro/specs/$1/ 2>/dev/null || echo ...' embeds user argument in bash -c without sanitization
suggested_fix: Sanitize $1 against ^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+$ before inline shell commands

description: rm -rf "$PARENT_DIR" where PARENT_DIR derives from user-supplied $1; empty-string guard prevents empty input but value is otherwise unrestricted, allowing deletion of arbitrary directories (e.g. $HOME, /tmp)
suggested_fix: Restrict PARENT_DIR to a safe temp prefix (e.g. require it starts with /tmp/ccmanager-) and reject paths containing .. or ~

description: curl fetches GitHub API to populate an ipset allowlist; compromised or manipulated API response could influence firewall rules; CIDR validation on lines 47-49 provides partial mitigation
suggested_fix: Add --max-time 10 --fail-with-body flags; pin the GitHub meta URL to HTTPS with certificate verification; consider caching known-good CIDR ranges as a fallback

description: devDependencies use ^ caret ranges allowing minor/patch version drift on fresh installs; could introduce breaking changes or compromised versions between lockfile updates
suggested_fix: Pin all devDependencies to exact versions or enable Dependabot/Renovate with approved-only merges

description: execFileSync passes { env: process.env } forwarding the complete calling-shell environment including any API keys or credentials to the platform-specific native binary
suggested_fix: Build an explicit env allowlist (PATH, HOME, TERM, COLORTERM, NO_COLOR) rather than passing all of process.env

description: CLAUDE.md lists npm run commands as the development interface but package.json scripts internally delegate to bun; bun is not listed as a prerequisite in CLAUDE.md, causing failures on systems without bun installed
suggested_fix: Add bun to the prerequisites section of CLAUDE.md and document the bun version requirement

description: Three commands (spec-requirements.md:21, spec-status.md:13, validate-gap.md:21) share the identical unsanitized !bash -c '$1' pattern; fix must be applied uniformly across all three to avoid partial remediation
suggested_fix: Extract a shared argument-validation header block or apply the sanitization pattern to all three commands in the same PR

description: 'Update' tool appears in allowed-tools of spec-design.md, spec-requirements.md, and spec-status.md but not in the other 7 commands; selective presence suggests an unfinished or inconsistent edit rather than intentional specialization
suggested_fix: Either remove Update from all three files (if it is not a real tool) or add it consistently to all commands that perform write operations
